task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the URL bar showing 'cameochemicals.noaa.gov' or the CAMEO Chemicals homepage is visible.

task_completion.description: Search for the required chemicals on the CAMEO website
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser tabs, search results, or chemical data pages showing queries for Acetone, Sulfuric Acid, Sodium Cyanide, Potassium Permanganate, and/or Sodium.

A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal window is open, showing the compilation of a report that includes the chemical names and placeholders or entries for UN/NA Number, DOT Hazard Class, and Packing Group.

task_completion.description: Record correct classification for Acetone and Sulfuric Acid
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explicitly lists Acetone with UN 1090, Class 3, PG II and Sulfuric Acid with UN 1830, Class 8, PG II.

task_completion.description: Record correct classification for Sodium Cyanide and Potassium Permanganate
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explicitly lists Sodium Cyanide with UN 1689, Class 6.1, PG I and Potassium Permanganate with UN 1490, Class 5.1, PG II.

task_completion.description: Record correct classification for Sodium and save the file to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text explicitly lists Sodium with UN 1428, Class 4.3, PG I, and there is evidence (like a save dialog, terminal command, or file manager view) that the file is saved as 'dot_shipping_report.txt' on the Desktop (~/Desktop).

integrity.description: Agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals website to retrieve the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the cameochemicals.noaa.gov website in a browser, rather than just typing the answers from its internal pre-training knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent created the report file properly
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a text editor (like gedit, mousepad, nano, vim) or standard shell commands (like echo or cat) to create the file, rather than executing a pre-existing script to generate the output.

integrity.description: Agent only recorded the requested fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The final report should focus on the requested UN/NA Number, DOT Hazard Class, and Packing Group, without hallucinating extra regulatory frameworks not requested in the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Acetone: UN 1090, Class 3, PG II. Sulfuric Acid: UN 1830, Class 8, PG II. Sodium Cyanide: UN 1689, Class 6.1, PG I. Potassium Permanganate: UN 1490, Class 5.1, PG II. Sodium: UN 1428, Class 4.3, PG I. The output file must be saved exactly at ~/Desktop/dot_shipping_report.txt.
